---
title: "Participant rate analysis"
output: html_document
date: "2026-04-26"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)
library(rmdformats)

## Global options
options(max.print="75")
# opts_chunk$set(echo=TRUE,
#                cache=TRUE,
#                prompt=FALSE,
#                tidy=TRUE,
#                comment=NA,
#                message=FALSE,
#                warning=FALSE)
# opts_knit$set(width=75)
```

```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(tidyverse)
library(readr)
library(lme4)
library(ggplot2)
library(stringr)
library(MuMIn)
library(sf)
library(tmap)
library(spdep)
library(mapview)
library(purrr)
library(cartogram)
library(spatialreg)

```

```{r}
data <- read_rds("your path to K2 mobility data")
data <- data[,-c(1)]

data <- data %>%
  rename(
    sending_city  = `Sending City`,
    receiving_city = `Receiving City`,
    sending_country  = `Sending Country`,
    receiving_country = `Receiving Country`
  )

# Extract ISO-2 codes from labels
data <- data %>%
  mutate(
    send_code = str_extract(sending_country,   "^[A-Z]{2}"),
    recv_code = str_extract(receiving_country, "^[A-Z]{2}")
  )

# Define European analysis universe (programme + key partner countries)
europe_codes <- c(
  "AL","AT","BE","BG","CH","CY","CZ","DE","DK","EE","ES","FI",
  "FR","GR","HR","HU","IE","IS","IT","LI","LT","LU","LV","MT",
  "NL","NO","PL","PT","RO","SE","SI","SK","TR","UK","ME","MK",
  "RS","UA","MD","XK"
)

# Keep only flows where both sending and receiving countries are European
data <- data %>%
  filter(
    send_code %in% europe_codes,
    recv_code %in% europe_codes,
    send_code != recv_code
  )
```


```{r}
gdp_data <- read.csv("your path to GDP data")

# Extract the GDP data for years 2014-2022
gdp_data <- gdp_data[gdp_data$TIME_PERIOD>=2014 & gdp_data$TIME_PERIOD<=2022,]

# Keep only the Euro value
gdp_data <- gdp_data%>%
  filter(
    na_item == 'B1GQ',
    unit == 'CP_MEUR'
  )
gdp_data <- gdp_data[,-c(1, 2, 3, 4, 5,9, 10)]

# Rename columns
 gdp_data <- gdp_data%>%
   rename('country' = 'geo',
          'year' = 'TIME_PERIOD',
          'gdp_value' = 'OBS_VALUE')
```


```{r}
# Although the file is saved as tsv the data inside the rows is formatted as CSV meaning that extra transformation is required to extreact the data
pop_data <- read_tsv("your path to population data")

# Read in the country names
names(pop_data)[1] <- c('country')

# split the file into CSV
pop_data <- pop_data %>%
  filter(str_detect(country,"A,NR,TOTAL,T"))
pop_data$country <- str_remove(pop_data$country,"A,NR,TOTAL,T,")

pop_data <- pop_data[, c(1, 56:64)]

# transform into usable datset
pop_data <- pop_data%>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = '2014':'2022',
    names_to = "year",
    values_to = "population"
  )

pop_data$year <- as.numeric(pop_data$year)

```
```{r}
# combine the GDP dataset and the Population dataset by country and year
 add_df <- pop_data%>%
   inner_join(gdp_data, by=c('country', 'year'))

# correct value format
add_df$gdp_value <- gsub("[^0-9.,]", "", add_df$gdp_value)
add_df$population <- gsub("[^0-9.,]", "",add_df$population)
add_df$gdp_value <- as.numeric(add_df$gdp_value)
add_df$population <- as.numeric(add_df$population)

# correct NA with data from Wikipedia
add_df$population[385:386] <- c(1793000, 1745000)

# calculate normalised GDP value
add_df$norm_gdp_val <- round((add_df$gdp_value/add_df$population)*100,2)

# rename columns
names(add_df)<- c("send_code", "year", "population_rec", "gdp_value_rec", "norm_gdp_val_rec")
```
 

```{r}
# join the three data sets
df <- data%>%
inner_join(add_df, by=c('year', 'send_code'))

# aggregate the data frame for participation
df_country <- df%>%
  count(send_code, year, na.rm = TRUE)
df_info <- df[,c(5,6,8,9,10)]
df_info <- unique(df_info)

df_country <- df_country%>%
  inner_join(df_info, by=c('year', 'send_code'))

# rename the iso code column
names(df_country)[1] <- c("CNTR_ID")

# calculate the participation rate
df_country$participation_rate <- ((df_country$n/df_country$population)*100)

```


```{r}
# add border polygons
polygon_data <- st_read("your path to the polygon border data")
df_country$CNTR_ID <- str_split_fixed(df_country$CNTR_ID, " ", 2)[,1]

geo_data <- polygon_data %>% 
  inner_join(df_country, by=c("CNTR_ID"))
```


```{r}
# plotting the raw participation rate for EU countries between the years 2019-2022
geo_data[geo_data$year %in% c("2019","2020","2021","2022"), ] %>%
  tm_shape() +
  tm_facets(by = "year") +
  tm_polygons(
    fill = "participation_rate",
    palette = c("#F67451", "#E6D460", "#00E078", "#73E7D8"),
    title = "Participation frequency"
  ) +
  tm_layout(
    legend.outside = TRUE,
    legend.outside.position = "bottom"
  ) +
  tm_tiles()

# Plotting proportional participation map
df_carto_all <- geo_data %>%
  split(.$year) %>%
  map(~ cartogram_cont(.x, weight = "participation_rate", itermax = 5)) %>%
  bind_rows()

ggplot(df_carto_all) +
  geom_sf(aes(fill = participation_rate)) +
  facet_wrap(~ year) +
  scale_fill_gradientn(
    colours = c("#064789", "#8CD790", "#FCFDBF", "#FE9F6D", "#DE4968"),
    values = scales::rescale(c(0, 0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 1))
  ) +
  labs(title="Proportional share of participation in Erasmus+", fill="share of participants")+
  theme_minimal()
```


```{r}
# Calculating the queen contiguity neighbourhoods
nb <- poly2nb(geo_data, queen = TRUE)
country_nb <- st_coordinates(st_centroid(geo_data$geom))

lw <- nb2listw(nb, style = "W", zero.policy = TRUE)

# Moran test for GDP and participation rate with Monte-Carlo simulation
moran.mc(geo_data$participation_rate, 
           lw,
           nsim = 999,
           zero.policy = TRUE)
moran.mc(geo_data$norm_gdp_val_rec, 
           lw,
           nsim = 999,
           zero.policy = TRUE)
```

```{r}
# Calculating univ LISA clusters for participation rate and the bv LISA clusters for participation and GDP
lisa_participation <- localmoran(geo_data$participation_rate,lw,
            na.action=na.fail,
        conditional=TRUE, alternative = "two.sided", mlvar=TRUE,
        spChk=NULL, adjust.x=FALSE, zero.policy=attr(lw, "zero.policy"))

bv_lisa_participation_GDP <- localmoran_bv(geo_data$participation_rate, geo_data$norm_gdp_val_rec,
           lw,nsim = 499, scale = TRUE, alternative="two.sided",
 iseed=1L, no_repeat_in_row=FALSE, zero.policy=attr(lw, "zero.policy"))
```


```{r}
# Plotting univ LISA clusters for participation rate and the bv LISA clusters for participation and GDP
geo_data$lisa_uni_cluster <- attr(lisa_participation, "quadr")[,'pysal']
geo_data$lisa_bv_cluster <- attr(bv_lisa_participation_GDP, "quadr")[,'pysal']

# univ LISA
tm_shape(geo_data)+
  tm_polygons("lisa_uni_cluster",
              palette = c("#064789", "#8CD790", "#FE9F6D", "#DE4968"),
              title = "LISA Clusters") +
  tm_layout(frame = FALSE)
# bv LISA
tm_shape(geo_data)+
  tm_polygons("lisa_bv_cluster",
              palette = c("#064789", "#8CD790", "#FE9F6D", "#DE4968"),
              title = "LISA Clusters") +
  tm_layout(frame = FALSE)
```

```{r}
# applying the SLX model to the data
GDP_participation_model01 <- lmSLX(participation_rate ~ norm_gdp_val_rec + year, data = geo_data, listw = lw)
summary(GDP_participation_model01)
```

```{r}
# applying the SEM model to the data
GDP_participation_model02 <- errorsarlm(participation_rate ~ norm_gdp_val_rec + year, data = geo_data, listw = lw)
summary(GDP_participation_model02)
```
